title: The One With All The Candy

lines.text: Central Park, Ross is teaching Ben how to ride a bike. Chandler, Monica, and Phoebe are they also.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: (on the bike) I'm ready!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You sure?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Uh-huh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, let me just straighten out your helmet there. (Does so.)

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: (To Chandler) Thanks daddy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no, one daddy, two mommies. All right, it's all yours. (Ross pushes him off.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes! Yes! Yes!! (Everyone stands and claps.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: His first big kid's bike, this is so exciting!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah, I remember mine! Ohh, it was my sixth birthday, my dad took me to the park, I got it, and...it bent.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I never had a bike of my own.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (shocked) What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, we didn't have a lot of money. But the girl across the street had the best bike! It was pink and it had rainbow colored tassels hanging off the handle grips, and-and-and a bell and this big, white wicker basket with those plastic daisies stuck on.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That sounds like my first bike. (They all turn and look at him.) My dad gave me his old one.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did the girl ever let you ride it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! But she gave me the box that it came in. It had a picture of the bike on the front. (They're all speechless) So I would sit on it and my step-dad would drag me around the backyard.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That is so unfair!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Not really, I got to drag him around too! (They all nod, "Oh.")
Opening Credits
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica is cooking, Joey is eating, and Chandler is entering from the bedroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! What are you guys doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Making holiday candy for the neighbors.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sorry, who?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm gonna hang this basket (Points to the one sitting on the table) on the door and when the neighbors walk by they can all take a piece.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But we don't know the neighbors.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I do. There's uh, let's see, Guy With a Mustache, Smokes-A-Lot Lady, Some Kids I've Seen, and A Red-haired Guy Who Does Not Like To Be Called Rusty.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: See? This is exactly why I'm making this candy. We can learn their names and get to know our neighbors.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wouldn't it be easier if we just moved?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering, happily) Gooood morning!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Eh, somebody's in a good mood!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, why shouldn't I be? I have great friends! I have a wonderful job!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Where you can make out with your assistant.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on, it's not a big deal! We stayed up all night coming up with a plan so that us dating will not be a problem.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah, what's the plan?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (pauses as she thinks and exhales loudly) We... We are not... ...going to let it... be a problem.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! It took you all night to come up with that plan?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well y'know, we did other stuff too. (Joey and Chandler start to giggle.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did you two...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Monica come on, y'know I don't sleep with guys on the first date!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Matt Guire, Mark Lynn, Ben Wire...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Anymore!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.
Rachel's Office, she's at her desk while the mailman delivers her mail and calls Tag in.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi. Tag, I have a conference call today is that correct?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Yes, at 4:00.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, thank you. That'll be all. (The mail guy leaves and Tag starts to follow, but Rachel stops him.) (Excitedly) Wait! Wait! (Rushes over and closes the office-door.) Did you see that? That mail guy had no idea there was something going on between us. (They kiss.)

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: I'd better get back to my desk.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you hard worker! I'll remember to put that in your evaluation.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: My what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, you've been here for two months now and your boss is required to hand in a performance evaluation. But y'know, there is one thing that I have yet to evaluate. (She turns around and sweeps the stuff off of her desk and hops up onto it.)

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Are you serious?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I've just always wanted to do that. Can you help me clean this up?
The Hallway, Monica and Chandler are returning and Monica finds her basket is empty.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The basket is totally empty! My God, the neighbors ate all the candy!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, either that or uh...(Motions towards Joey's door.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey!!
She storms into his apartment to find him with a towel around his shoulders, a bowl on his head, and Phoebe with scissors in her hand.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did you eat all the neighbor candy?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh well yeah, that was the plan, but by the time I got to it there was only a couple of pieces left!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, and they've been coming by all day. They love it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They love my candy? Oh man!!! I've gotta go make more!! (Starts to do so.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Mon, you might wanna make some more lasagna too, because something might've happened to a huge chunk of it.
Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross! The neighbors ate all my candy!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mine stole my newspaper! It's like a crime wave!! (Monica runs to make more candy.) Pheebs, you uh, you got a second.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, ever since you uh, told me that story about that bike I-I couldn't stop thinking about it. I mean, everyone should have a-a first bike, so...
He goes out into the hall and re-enters with a bike exactly like the one Phoebe described earlier.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (gasps) Oh my God Ross!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You like it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I love it!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh!! Ohh!! And I love you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ahh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Not that way! But the bike brought you a lot closer!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah! (She hugs him.) Well uh-uh, t-take it downstairs, y'know give it a test ride.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay! Oh my God! My first bike! Thank you for the best present I've ever gotten.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (stunned at the complement) You're welcome.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (starts to leave, but stops) Oh and Chandler's about to cry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (about to cry) Am not!
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, the middle of the night, there is someone knocking on the door and Monica and Chandler get up to answer it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (turning a light on) Who is that?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't worry, I'm brave! I am brave! I...I am brave! (They get to the door and Monica goes to open it.) No-no-no-no!! (He stops her.) (Through the door.) Can you tell me who is there please?

lines.speaker: The Knocker
lines.text: My name is Gary, I live upstairs.
Monica opens the door.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi. Do you know what time it is?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: It's candy time! My roommate says that they taste like little drops of heaven.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh please! (To Chandler) Did you hear that? Little drops of heaven.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (laughs) 4:00 A.M.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: So, can I get some candy?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I am sorry, but some of us have to get up early and go to work! (Monica looks at him) (To Monica) He does not know that I am not some of us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, listen I am sorry, but I'll put some out first thing in the morning.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Well okay, I'll swing by later. Do you live in this building?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Um-hmm.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: (looking at Monica.) Mm! Seems like I would've remembered you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Mm! Night Gar'! (Monica closes the door.)
Rachel's Office, she's slinking out to where Tag works and checks to make sure no one is coming.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (hugging him) So did you read your evaluation yet?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: No! It was marked confidential I just sent it down to Human Resources.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (worried) Okay please, you're kidding right?! I wrote that one as a joke for you!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: A joke they would appreciate?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm thinkin' no.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: What did you say?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm, I said I thought you were a good kisser, and uh, and that I like your tiney-tiny touchie.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: No, not my touchie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, it gets worse. When asked if you take initiative I wrote, "Yes, he was able to unhook my bra with minimal supervision," and under Problems with Performance I wrote, "Dear God, I hope not," and then uh, then I drew a little smiley face, and then a small pornographic sketch.
Central Perk, Joey, Monica, Ross, and Chandler are watching Phoebe polish the daisies on her bike outside.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross! That is so sweet of you to get Phoebe that bike! When I heard the story, I almost cried.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Almost cried huh? Hear that Chandler? Almost cried!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you cry every time somebody talks about Titanic!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (about to cry) Those two only had each other!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe really likes the bike huh?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah! I saw her walkin' it down the street the other day. She had uh, these flowers in the basket. It was so cute.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I saw her this morning walkin' it by the park.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait a minute, she was walking the bike? Both times?
[Cut to outside, they're going to talk to Phoebe about walking the bike.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! So are you enjoying the bike?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, uh-huh so much!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pheebs you uh...you do know how to ride a bike don't you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Of course!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, can we see you ride it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (The gang is giggles then Phoebe gets the bike out of the rack, gets on, pushes off, rolls a few feet, and falls over.) See?
Commercial Break
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica is making candy while Chandler and Rachel are taste testing and Joey is on the couch doodling.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: See, this is why I told you never get involved with your assistant! And here is no such thing as keeping secrets when it comes to affairs. (To Chandler) Did you hear that Chandler? No such thing!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (with his mouth full) What happened? I'm just eating candy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Maybe it's not as bad as I think. Y'know, maybe they didn't take it the way I meant it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Absolutely! Y'know, because touchie can mean both ass and good worker.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh, I just gotta get the thing back!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That sketch you mentioned? Might it have looked a little something like this? (He shows her what he's been drawing.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Joey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What is the matter with you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Boy I tell ya, this little talent came in handy before I could afford porn.
There is a knock on the door and Monica answers it. There is a woman standing there.

lines.speaker: The Woman
lines.text: Hi! I'm sorry, I know it's after hours but I really need candy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry, I can't help you. See? (Points to her new sign.) Rules are rules.

lines.speaker: The Woman
lines.text: Please! I have people coming from out of town today! And, I told them all about your candy!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No kidding, out of towners huh? What did you tell 'em?

lines.speaker: The Woman
lines.text: I told 'em your candy is absolutely indescribable!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Some people have been saying its y'know little drops of heaven, but whatever.

lines.speaker: The Woman
lines.text: Please, can't you help me out?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (watching the discussion) Hey Chandler, do we know that lady?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Maybe, isn't she the woman who lives below you and has sex really loud?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (still talking to the woman) All right, I'll do it just this once! But you can't tell anybody!

lines.speaker: The Woman
lines.text: (exasperatedly) Yes! Yes! Please, just give it to me!

lines.speaker: Joey and Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, that's her.

lines.speaker: The Woman
lines.text: (after Monica gives her the candy) Thank you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (closing the door) It's unbelievable! I-I can't believe that sign didn't work!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what would work?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hmm?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Stop making candy!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But they like it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mean they like you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Maybe.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is that why you became a chef? So that people would like you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, you really want to talk about getting people to like you huh, funny man? (Joey laughs but stops when Chandler turns to glare at him.)
Central Park, Ross is teaching Phoebe how to ride her bike.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, now just remember everything I taught you and you'll be fine. Okay? Here we go. Ready...Set...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (hopping off the bike) Wait! This seat is really uncomfortable! Yeah, maybe before we start we should just get another one. Perhaps, like an airplane seat-or a beanbag chair!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, you can't get out of this! Okay? You have to learn how to ride a bike!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why? Why do I have to learn?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well...In-in case of emergency.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What kind of an emergency?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well let there-what if a man comes along and puts a gun to your head and says, "You ride this bike or I'll sh...I'll shoot you."

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I would ring the bell to distract him and then I would knock the gun out of his hand with a Chinese throwing star.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, Phoebe just-just get-get on the bike and-Hey! I'll hold you up and-and push you. Okay?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You won't let go?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Swear?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I swear!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (thinks it over) Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on. (She gets on the bike.) All right, here we go. All right? (They start.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right. Feel good?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, try pedaling.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (Does so.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's it, your doing great.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Doing great! Yes-yes-yes! Take control! Yes!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Weee!!! (Ross pushes her and lets go.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes!! Yes!!! (He starts clapping and Phoebe turns around to look at him and falls off the bike.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Oh! Oh no!! You swore!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I-I just thought you were doing so well. I...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I am shocked! Shocked!! (She storms off leaving Ross to defend himself from the angry looks from onlookers.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's a legitimate learning technique. (Looking around and seeing the woman behind him glaring at him.) Wow!
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica is making candy like mad as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, there's uh, some people outside, askin' about candy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, they're just gonna have to wait aren't they? I've only got two hands!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Need some help?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! You don't know the system! There'll be nobody messing with the system!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: By the way, the week before your wedding you may not see a lot of me. (She glares at him and he quickly makes his way to Phoebe's room.)
[Cut to Phoebe's room, Phoebe has put her helmet on one of her teddy bears and is playing around with it. Ross knocks and pokes his head in the door.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (seeing him) Oh, hello liar.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Look, I-I'm really sorry I let go of the bike.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I could've been killed I hope you know!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know. I know. But, can we please try it again? Huh? I mean, you were so close Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I would love to but...the bike got stolen and the police have no suspects. (Ross just happens to have his hand on a sheet that is covering something that suspiciously looks like a bike.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! (Ross rings the bell.) What the hell?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, y'know what? If you are not going to learn how to ride this bike then I'm sorry, I'm just gonna have to take it back.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! Why?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Because! Because, it-it-it's... It'd be like you having this guitar (Points to hers) and-and never playing it. Okay, this guitar wants to be played! And-and this bike wants to be ridden and-and if you don't ride it you-you're-you're killing its spirit! (Pause) The bike is dying.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right. If you care enough to make up that load of crap, okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Great! Great! (He runs to the door.) You're making the bike very happy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (sarcastically) Okay Ross! (Ross exits and she says quietly to the bike) Please don't die!
Rachel's office, Tag and her are planning how to get the review back.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, I think we can get the evaluation back before they see it, but we're gonna have to get into Mr. Zelner's office. Now, he doesn't get in until 10, so he's no problem, but his assistant, Betty, she comes in early to eat her breakfast at her desk.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: That's kinda sad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, well Betty's kinda sad. Which is why I believe I can lure her away with these chocolates. (Holding up the box of chocolates.) Now, while I distract her, you get in the office.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Got it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Let's roll!
They start towards the door but are stopped when Mr. Zelner enters.

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: Hello Rachel, you uh, got a minute?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (worried and shocked) Yeah, sure Mr. Zelner, for you anything-minute. Okay. Fine. (To Tag) Abort the plan, abort the plan. (She start to usher Tag out.)

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: Uh actually, I'd like to speak with both of you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Uh, well can we, can we get you anything Mr. Zelner? Maybe some chocolates? (Holds up the box.)

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: Umm, no. Thanks, but I'll give these to Betty. (Rachel glances at Tag to say, "See?") So I read your evaluation of Tag, or to use his full name, Tag Sweetcheeks Jones. Is something going on with you two?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughs) Oh my God. Can you imagine if there was?! I mean, (getting serious) what would happen exactly.

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: Well, I'd be forced to file a report. I'd have to consult with the legal department, and your future at the company would be in jeopardy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: (interrupting) Uh Mr. Zelner, I'm the one who filled in that evaluation.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no-no-no...

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: (interrupting again) Yeah-yeah-yeah-yeah-yeah, I thought it would be funny.

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: You wrote that you have a cute touchie?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: (pause) Yes. I have a weird sense of humor, and I'm kinda strangely proud of my butt.

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: It's kind of a risky joke Tag, and what is-what is this drawing I can't figure out what this is?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You're lookin' at it upside down-y'know what? (Grabs the evaluation and throws it out.) It doesn't matter.

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: Yeah, it's not like I don't have a sense of humor, huh? Hell, I even enjoy a naughty limerick now and then. But there's a time and a place, huh?! Unless you uh, have a limerick right now? (They both nod no.) No? Okay, well uh, you've (Grabs the chocolates.) got my fax number. (Exits.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa! I can't believe you did that. That was really sweet.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: No, don't worry about it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, you could've lost your job.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Are you kidding me?! With a cute butt like this, I'd find work.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you! You're great! (They kiss.)

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Y'know what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: I feel great. (Rachel laughs) In fact! (Walks over to her desk, sweeps its contents onto the floor and Rachel just glares at him.) What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I... It just-it took me so long to get that desk organized.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry. (He bends over to pick the stuff up.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looking at his butt) There it is. (They hug.)
The Hallway, Chandler is returning from work to see the hallway jammed full of people waiting outside their door.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (asking a man leaning against the wall) What is going on?

lines.speaker: The Man
lines.text: We're waiting for the candy. (Yelling at the door.) Bring out the candy!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah lady! Give us candy!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's up buddy?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Waiting for candy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Get in here! (They head for the door and Chandler sees Smokes-A-Lot Lady standing next to the door and smoking, to her) Hey, and you can not smoke in here! (Takes the cigarette and takes a drag for himself.) (Exhaling in ecstasy) Merry Christmas.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (opening the door and frantically) Okay, guys! The candy is coming; I just need another 15 minutes for the chocolate to cool!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: We want candy! We want candy now! (And other general commotion sounds.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right everybody! Just be quiet! Be quiet! Be quiet!! Pipe-pipe-pipe down! (They settle down) What is the matter with you people?! This woman was trying to do a nice thing for you. She was making candy so she could try to get to know all of you, and I'll bet that not one of you can tell me her name! Am I right?

lines.speaker: The Man
lines.text: (from earlier) Candy Lady?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No not (imitating) Candy Lady.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (stepping in and knocking the man over) Hey, if we know it can we have candy?!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, y'know what? Forget it, all of you forget it! You've ruined it! Go home! You've ruined it! You've ruined it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's right, it's all ruined! You guys ruined everything! You ruined it! (Steps into the apartment and Chandler closes the door.) (Joey struts over to the candy and starts eating it.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Chandler) Thank you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're welcome. (They kiss.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did you smoke?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! Smokes-A-Lot Lady blew smoke directly into my mouth. Eh-uh-are you okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm fine now, but it was really scary there for a while. I mean, someone slipped a-a threatening note under the door.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (with his mouth full) Oh yeah, sorry about that. Mob mentality or whatever, I don't know... (Grabs the note.)
Ending Credits
Central Park, Phoebe is now riding her bike with ease and stops in front of Ross. They both giggle.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't believe it! I did it! I rode a bike! I never thought I'd be able to do that! Thank you Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh hey, don't thank me, thank yourself. You're the one who faced her fears and ultimately overcame them.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don't be so corny Ross, it's not an after-school special. (She rides off and the camera pans down to reveal two shiny, silver training wheels firmly attached.)
End
